homepage.heroSubline: Precision welding for Michigan's toughest conditions. Serving Detroit and surrounding areas with durable solutions built to last.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Apex Welding started as a two-man operation repairing industrial equipment in Detroit's auto plants. Today, we're a 20-person crew tackling everything from historic home restorations to commercial fabrication.
We weld like Detroiters work - with stubborn attention to detail. Every joint gets our full focus, whether it's reinforcing a Corktown loft's steel beams or repairing salt-damaged truck frames.
When Michigan winters wreck metal, we fix it right. From donating skills to local schools to training the next generation of welders, we're invested in this community's strength.

valueProps.title: Weather-Tested
valueProps.description: Our welds withstand Detroit's freeze-thaw cycles and corrosive road salt year after year.

valueProps.title: Period-Appropriate
valueProps.description: We match techniques to your structure's era, whether it's a 1920s bungalow or modern loft.

valueProps.title: No Shortcuts
valueProps.description: Every project gets full penetration welds - we don't do cosmetic fixes that fail.

valueProps.title: Same-Day Quotes
valueProps.description: Get a firm price when we assess your job, not days later.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-WEL-RQ13 today for welding that stands up to Michigan winters.

services.name: Structural Steel Repair
services.shortDescription: Restore compromised structural steel in Detroit's aging buildings.
services.description:
Decades of freeze-thaw cycles and road salt corrosion take their toll on Detroit's structural steel. We specialize in repairing load-bearing beams, columns, and connections in mid-century factories, loft conversions, and commercial buildings.

Our process starts with ultrasonic testing to identify hidden corrosion. We then remove compromised sections with precision cutting before welding in new ASTM A36 steel plate reinforcements. All repairs meet Michigan building code requirements for historic structures.

Unlike patch jobs that fail within seasons, our full-penetration welds integrate new steel with existing members for decades of service. We've reinforced everything from Palmer Woods mansion supports to Midtown loft floor systems.
process: Step 1: Non-destructive testing to map corrosion and stress points
Step 2: Engineering-approved removal plan for compromised sections
Step 3: Custom fabrication of reinforcement plates on-site
Step 4: High-strength welding with post-weld heat treatment when required

faq.question: How do you prevent heat distortion in thin steel?
faq.answer: We use pulsed MIG welding with precise heat control, often working in 2-inch sections with cooling periods. For critical applications, we employ specialized backstep techniques.

faq.question: Can you match original weld appearances?
faq.answer: Yes. Our team studies period-appropriate techniques - from 1940s stick welds to modern flux-core - to maintain visual continuity in historic properties.

services.name: Residential Metal Fabrication
services.description:
From ornamental ironwork to functional solutions, we fabricate metal components that complement Southeast Michigan's housing stock. Our shop handles everything from wrought iron gates for Indian Village estates to steel lintel replacements for brick bungalows.

We understand how Michigan's climate affects residential metal. That's why we use thicker 11-gauge steel for exterior railings instead of flimsy 14-gauge, and why all our exterior welds get zinc-rich primers before painting. For historic districts, we replicate original profiles using archival photos.

Recent projects include custom steel basement window wells that resist frost heave, copper-clad bay window caps for Colonial revivals, and reinforced garage headers for heavy snow loads in Rochester Hills.
process: Step 1: On-site measurements and material selection
Step 2: CAD drawings for client approval
Step3: Precision cutting and forming in our Detroit shop
Step4: Installation with weatherproof sealing

faq.question: Do you work with historic commissions?
faq.answer: Yes. We've completed approved projects in Indian Village and Rosedale Park historic districts, following all guidelines for visible modifications.

faq.question: How long do exterior steel elements last?
faq.answer: With proper coating systems, our steel fabrications typically last 30+ years in Detroit's climate - we use industrial-grade finishes, not residential paint.

services.name: Automotive Welding
services.shortDescription: Expert repairs for Michigan's salt-ravaged vehicles.
services.description:
Road salt eats Michigan cars alive. We specialize in structural repairs that go beyond quick patches - properly reinforcing frame rails, suspension mounts, and unibody sections so your vehicle stays safe.

Our team welds everything from classic car restoration projects to daily drivers with rusted-out rocker panels. We only use automotive-grade steels and proper penetration techniques, never relying on bondo or temporary fixes. For vintage vehicles, we maintain original factory weld patterns.

Common repairs include full cab corner replacements on trucks, torque box reinforcements for SUVs, and proper floorpan repairs that don't compromise crash safety. We serve many local fleets and municipal vehicles that take brutal winter punishment.
process: Step 1: Media blasting to reveal full damage extent
Step 2: Precision cutting of compromised metal
Step 3: Fabrication of replacement panels
Step 4: GMAW welding with corrosion-resistant primers

faq.question: Can you match factory spot welds?
faq.answer: Yes. We use specialized resistance welders to replicate OEM appearance on restoration projects, with proper spacing and heat settings.

faq.question: How do you prevent future rust?
faq.answer: All repairs get zinc-rich weld-through primer, cavity wax injections, and undercoating - the same process we use on snowplows.

services.name: Commercial Welding
services.shortDescription: Industrial-strength solutions for Metro Detroit businesses.
services.description:
Keeping Michigan businesses running requires metalwork that can take punishment. We fabricate and repair everything from loading dock bumpers to conveyor systems for Detroit-area manufacturers, warehouses, and retailers.

Our commercial clients rely on us for emergency repairs when equipment fails - we've field-welded broken forklift masts at 2AM and reconstructed salt-damaged dock levelers before morning shifts. Preventative work includes reinforcing pallet rack uprights and building custom steel machine guards.

We understand the demands of local industries. That's why our shop welds use higher-grade materials than typical construction work, with proper preheat for Michigan's cold winters. All structural work includes engineer-stamped calculations.
process: Step 1: Immediate response for emergency repairs
Step 2: Engineering analysis for structural modifications
Step 3: Fabrication with industrial-grade materials
Step 4: Installation minimizing business disruption

faq.question: Do you handle food-grade stainless work?
faq.answer: Yes. Our certified welders perform sanitary stainless welding for commercial kitchens and food processors, with proper purging and finishing.

faq.question: Can you reinforce existing steel structures?
faq.answer: Absolutely. We've added capacity to warehouse mezzanines, dock bumpers, and overhead crane systems throughout Wayne County.

services.name: Historic Metal Restoration
services.description:
Detroit's historic buildings contain irreplaceable metal craftsmanship. We specialize in conserving and replicating original ornamental ironwork, structural elements, and decorative features using period-appropriate techniques.

Our restoration projects range from repairing Arts & Crafts era bronze door hardware in Palmer Woods to reconstructing missing wrought iron balcony railings on Midtown apartments. We document original work through metallurgical analysis before beginning repairs.

For replacement elements, we match original alloys and fabrication methods - hand-forging when required, using correct joint details, and duplicating aging patterns. All work meets National Park Service standards for historic preservation.
process: Step 1: Historic documentation and material sampling
Step 2: Conservation assessment of existing elements
Step 3: Custom fabrication matching original methods
Step 4: Installation with reversible preservation techniques

faq.question: Can you recreate missing historic elements?
faq.answer: Yes. Using archival photos and surviving examples, we've reconstructed everything from 1920s elevator grilles to Victorian fence finials for Detroit landmarks.

faq.question: Do you work with preservation architects?
faq.answer: Regularly. We're approved vendors for several firms specializing in Detroit historic renovations and adaptive reuse projects.

services.name: Emergency Welding
services.description:
When metal fails at the worst time, we respond. Our emergency team handles urgent structural repairs throughout Wayne and Oakland Counties, day or night.

We've stabilized collapsed carport roofs after heavy snow loads, temporarily braced broken water main valves, and field-repaired snowplow blades during storms. Our mobile welding rig carries equipment for on-site stainless, aluminum, and steel repairs in all conditions.

Emergency services include immediate stabilization of dangerous situations, followed by permanent repairs once safe. We coordinate with local building departments when required and provide documentation for insurance claims.
process: Step 1: Immediate safety assessment
Step 2: Temporary stabilization if needed
Step 3: Permanent repair planning
Step 4: Documentation for authorities/insurance

faq.question: What's your average response time?
faq.answer: Within 90 minutes for life-safety situations in our core service area, often faster for municipal clients.

faq.question: Can you weld in the rain or snow?
faq.answer: Yes. Our rig has weather enclosures and we use specialized procedures for wet conditions - we don't postpone critical repairs.

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: Apex saved our Corktown loft building when an inspection revealed severe beam corrosion. They engineered a solution that preserved the historic character while bringing everything up to code. Two winters later, their welds are holding perfect.

reviews.name: Danielle K.
reviews.text: Our Indian Village home needed period-appropriate steel casement window frames replicated. Apex studied original 1927 examples, then fabricated perfect matches down to the original weld bead pattern. The preservation commission approved them without changes.

reviews.name: Rob S.
reviews.text: After three shops told me my '68 Mustang's frame rails were too far gone, Apex methodically replaced 18 inches of structural steel using correct resistance spot welds. The car drives straight and the repair is invisible to judges at shows.

reviews.name: Lena P.
reviews.text: When our bakery's conveyor oven frame cracked during peak holiday production, Apex had a crew onsite in 45 minutes. They reinforced the entire structure overnight with food-grade stainless - we only lost 4 hours of baking time.

reviews.name: James R.
reviews.text: Christmas Eve snow collapse took out our carport. Apex arrived at 10PM with temporary supports, then returned Dec 26 with a permanent solution using heavier steel. Their quick action saved our vehicles from further damage.

reviews.name: Theresa M.
reviews.text: Restoring our 1915 Arts & Crafts building's bronze storefront was daunting until we found Apex. Their metallurgist identified the original alloy, and their artisans recast missing elements using century-old techniques. The results are museum-quality.
